# Migrating Legacy Tests to Swift Testing

Use this guide when migrating tests generated by earlier template versions that still follow **Quick + Nimble** conventions.

**Authoritative API references:** [Swift Testing](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/testing), [XCTest](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/xctest). WWDC24: [Meet Swift Testing](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2024/10179/), [Go further with Swift Testing](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2024/10195/).

---

## Typical Quick + Nimble patterns

| Pattern | Role |
|--------|------|
| `final class FooSpec: QuickSpec` / `AsyncSpec` | One spec type per file or feature, using `override class func spec() { ... }` |
| `describe("...")` | Group tests by type or feature |
| `context("...")` | Sub-group tests by scenario |
| `it("...")` | Describe a single behavior or assertion group |
| `beforeEach` / `afterEach` | Per-suite or per-context setup and teardown |
| `expect(a).to(equal(b))`, `beTrue()`, `beNil()` | Assertions |
| `await expect(...).toEventually(...)` | Async or main-actor expectations in `AsyncSpec` |
| Nested helpers inside `spec()` | File-scoped test helpers |
| `@MainActor` on `AsyncSpec` subclasses | View model or UI-related unit tests |
| `@testable import ModuleName` | Access internal types under test |

---

## Mapping Quick and Nimble to Swift Testing

| Quick / Nimble | Swift Testing |
|----------------|----------------|
| `describe("Subject") { }` | `@Suite("Subject")` on a `struct`, or a display name on a suite |
| `context("...") { }` | Nested `@Suite` types, or separate `@Suite` types in the same file |
| `it("does X") { }` | `@Test("does X") func ...` |
| `expect(x).to(equal(y))` | `#expect(x == y)` |
| `expect(x).to(beNil())` | `#expect(x == nil)` or `let x = try #require(optional)` |
| `expect { ... }.to(throwError())` | `await #expect(throws: ...) { ... }` |
| `beforeEach` | `init()` on a suite type, or explicit setup at the start of each `@Test` |
| `afterEach` | `defer` in the test, or a helper |
| `AsyncSpec` with `async` tests | `async` / `throws` on `@Test` methods |
| `@MainActor` on a spec class | `@MainActor` on the suite or individual tests |
| Local helpers inside `spec()` | `private` / `fileprivate` functions, or a small namespace type |

**Parameterized cases**:

```swift
@Test(arguments: [value1, value2, value3])
func behaviorHolds(for input: InputType) {
    #expect(...)
}
```

**Tags**:

```swift
extension Tag {
    @Tag static var integration: Tag
}

@Test(.tags(.integration))
func networkRequestDecodesResponse() async throws { ... }
```

---

## Naming and folder updates

| Legacy | Recommended |
|--------|-------------|
| `HomeViewModelSpec.swift` | `HomeViewModelTests.swift` |
| `final class HomeViewModelSpec: AsyncSpec` | `struct HomeViewModelTests { }` or `@Suite("HomeViewModel") struct ...` |
| `Tests/Sources/Specs/<Feature>/...` | `Tests/Sources/<Feature>/...` |

Avoid using `Spec` in test file names, suite names, test names, and folder paths.

---

## Migration checklist

1. Replace `QuickSpec` / `AsyncSpec` with a **`struct`** and `@Suite` / `@Test`.
2. Map `describe` / `context` to nested `@Suite` types or clear `@Test` titles.
3. Replace `expect` with `#expect` / `#require`.
4. Move `beforeEach` / `afterEach` into `init()`, `defer`, or helpers.
5. Preserve `@MainActor` where the old test suite relied on it.
6. Ensure the app target has `ENABLE_TESTABILITY` enabled for Debug-style configs when using `@testable import`.
